observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Require the inventory committee to timely perform their duties or
functions in conducting an actual physical count of PPEs for a specified
period, to facilitate proper accounting and reporting and correct
classification of fixed properties; and submit a complete and more
reliable RPCPPE; and
b) Direct the GSO-designate or her delegate to ensure that the PPEs listed
in the RPCPPE are properly backed up with their pertinent AREs/PARs
and are properly kept, filed, and secured within their office premises.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to conduct a periodic physical
count of inventories and to prepare and submit the RPCI, RPSCP, and
other necessary forms and registries to properly account the remaining
inventory balance at year-end;
b) Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the discrepancies in their respective inventory records; and
c) Ensure that all offices strictly adhere to Section 2 of COA Circular No.
97-002 and strengthen the internal control system on procurements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Accountant to prepare a revised Bank Reconciliation
Statement per individual bank account for the Trust Fund;
b) Acting Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the necessary measures to ensure
that the balance of LBP bank account number 0041-2982-51 will now be
recorded in the books and to stop further incurrence of bank charges and
recover the previously charged amounts to LBP bank account number
0042-1325-00; and
c) Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to conduct, at least, quarterly
reconciliation of their records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Reconcile the negative balances noted and the unliquidated balances from
prior years, as well as send demand letters to the AOs with unliquidated
balances; and
b) Ensure that no additional cash advances will be granted to AOs with
outstanding balances, in compliance with Section 89 of PD 1445.

We also recommended that the Management revisit their policies as far as
granting of cash advances is concerned and consider designating additional Special
Disbursing Officers (SDO) to ensure that no multiple cash advances will be granted
despite of unliquidated balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Conduct in-depth evaluation to identify the veracity, nature and claim of
the recorded payables, particularly those which remain unsettled for
more than three to 20 years already to determine which obligations have
valid claimants;
b) Take up the appropriate reversions/adjustments in the books of the
Municipality for those transactions proven to be without valid claimants
and/or dormant accounts for fair presentation of liabilities in the
financial statements;
c) For those transactions still being pursued by claimants, exert efforts in
retrieving the applicable supporting documents for the recorded payables
to check their validity and allow for their subsequent settlements; and
d) In ensuing periods, ensure that that the journal entry vouchers recording
accounts payable at year-end are supported with complete documentation
to evidence the validity and legality of the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Review the records pertaining to prior years to reconcile the accounts as
well as ensure that remittances are made in full and intact for all taxes
and contributions withheld; and
b) Inquire with the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation regarding the
unpaid premium for January 2018 reflected in their statement of account
and ensure that such will be resolved.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the non-moving previous year’s
balances in order to properly identify their status and make the necessary actions
such as creating an adjusting entry or returning the fund to the Source Agency.

We recommended that the LGU revisit their duties and responsibilities
specified in the MOA with the ECCDC and ensure the availability of water supply
and prioritization of construction of a perimeter fence, as required by the latter.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer to conduct an investigation on the noted damaged ceiling of the covered
façade and effect the necessary repair.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to conduct a thorough review and reconciliation of the transactions
recorded under the Other Payables account to ensure proper and accurate financial
recording.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Formulate and adopt measures to intensify revenue collection efforts by
strictly enforcing existing tax laws and ordinances within the
Municipality; and
b) Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure the proper conduct of
periodic assessment/evaluation of collection accomplishments in order to
warrant the tapping of all sources to maximize the LGU’s revenue-
generating powers as mandated by law.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office, Municipal Budget Office,
and Municipal Engineering Officer to continue its collective efforts in fully utilizing
its 20 percent LDF and to prompt the Municipal Development Council to
meticulously identify implementation-ready projects as priority development
projects to ensure that projects to be accomplished during the year are implemented
as scheduled.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Direct the MDC to thoroughly evaluate the proposed priority
development projects to be funded under the LDF as to their readiness
for implementation or procurement to avoid delays or non-
implementation thereof;
b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the savings from the
completed projects to the General Fund; and
c) Direct the MDC, MBO, MEO, and other concerned offices to ensure
well-planned development projects and clear and consistent project titles
and descriptions across all documents to prevent misinterpretation of the
project.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Direct the concerned offices of the Municipality of Gainza to continue its
collective effort in prioritizing the implementation of the planned PPAs
funded under the LDRRM in order to enhance the disaster-preparedness
and response of the Municipality and deliver timely and beneficial
services to the public;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and LDRRMO to include in the
LDRRM Utilization Report as part of the available fund sources, the
financial assistance received from various sources totaling ₱380,000.00;
and
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to
conduct periodic reconciliation of their records on the appropriations
and utilizations of the LDRRM funds so that the accurate amounts are
disclosed in the reports and financial statements at year-end.

We recommended and the Management agreed:
a) Ensure the execution of SB Ordinance No. 11-2023 dated December 4,
2023 on the implementation of a comprehensive and sustainable GAD
program, creating the implementing mechanisms thereof, and providing
funds for the purpose;
b) Ascertain that the PPAs to be included in the GPB are properly and
judiciously planned to ensure feasibility and implementation of said
projects and that cost of implementation of activities for the year totaled
at least five percent of the annual budget; and
c) Ensure proper, consistent, and clear documentation in the GPB, GAD
AR, DVs and their supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to prepare the correcting entry for the cash
donation received amounting to ₱380,000.00; and
b) MDRRMO to coordinate with the PVB and LBP formally create a
MOA/MOU, or at least secure a certification, to specifically outline the
donors’ intent.

instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prospectively furnish the Municipal Accountant, at the beginning of the year, a duly certified list of the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and
require the Municipal Accountant to record the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year to avoid understatement of receivable accounts and misrepresentation of the financial statements, as a whole.

strictly require the Municipal Accountant to prepare bank reconciliation statements in an opportune time for all funds of the Municipality; 

instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers per bank account under Trust Fund for proper monitoring and reconciliation with the depository banks; and

direct the Municipal Accountant to investigate and validate the confirmed balance of the LGU’s General Fund with the management of LBP.

undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the payable’s accounts particularly the previous years recorded payable of ₱0.76 million which are already outstanding for more than three years to twelve years, and if found to have no valid claims against the local government unit, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus. Otherwise, if there are still claims for the long outstanding payables, notify the supplier and the end user to attach the necessary supporting documents for the payment of the same;

ensure the observance of Section 119 and Section 45 of PD 1445 that recording be based on the delivery of goods or services and substantiated with sufficient evidence in setting up a liability to make sure that the recorded accounts payable are valid obligations of the Municipality; and

review all recorded Accounts Payable made at the end of the year which were only based on the obligation request to determine all those subject to adjustment in order to effect the same.

Continue formulating responsive programs and projects to properly utilize the funds appropriated by the LGU and implement the same in accordance to the Annual and Supplemental Budgets approved by the Sangguniang Bayan; and 

Ensure that projects, programs, activities and expenses implemented by the LGU will not exceed the appropriated amounts as found in the Annual and Supplemental Budgets.

a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prospectively furnish the Municipal

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain an updated Cashbook for Cash in
Bank-LCCA account and it shall be readily available for inspection anytime
during office hours;
b) instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
records and observe the highest standards of prudence and consistency in the
keeping of accounts as safeguard against inaccurate or misleading information
to stakeholders; and
c) require the Municipal Accountant to complete the Bank Reconciliation
Statements as of yearend.

We recommended that Management:
a) require the Accountable Officers concerned to settle/liquidate immediately
their long outstanding cash advances and the concerned officials to adhere
strictly to the rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation
of cash advances provided under COA Circular 97-002;
b) require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters and institute
appropriate measures to enforce full liquidation of cash advances;
c) ensure that outstanding cash advances are liquidated prior to separation from
office by any appointed or elected officer or employee.

We recommended that Management:
a.) direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze all receivables that are
dormant, long outstanding and unsubstantiated for proper disposition and
monitoring;
b.) direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers and
supporting documents to support the recorded balance
c.) intensify collection strategies and exhaust all possible means to enforce
payment from the debtors.
d.) initiate effort to request authority from the COA to write off long dormant
receivable that are deemed uncollectible after all remedies have been
exhausted.

We recommended that Management:
a) direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit a duly certified list of the name of
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in accordance with
Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs to serve as basis for the
prospective recording of RPT and SET Receivables;
b) enjoin the Municipal Accountant to record the SET Receivables and Deferred
SET Income for CY 2019 amounting to ₱442,781.24 fairly present the
financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
a) review the existing practices on inventory management and implement the
Perpetual Inventory Method System in accounting for the receipts and
issuances of supplies and materials required under the NGAS Manual for
LGUs;
b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the pertinent Stock Cards for
inventory items within and without their custody on the basis of AIRs for
proper monitoring of the movement of inventory items;
c) enforce to the heads of offices/departments the immediate submission of
relevant supporting documents upon distribution or consumption of the
inventories under their custody for the timely preparation of SSMI by the
Municipal Treasurer;
d) instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of
supplies and materials by type and prepare the Report of the Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI), subject to reconciliation between records of
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer; and
e) enjoin the Municipal Accountant to stop from crediting Inventories
account without the documentary requirements supporting actual
consumption or distribution of inventories.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor together with the Sangguniang
Bayan explain in writing why the chief executive did not represent the Municipality
of Gainza in the contract of agreement for the purchase of the lot costing
₱1,063,829.79 and immediately submit the lacking documents to make the payment
made to PDIC-FAO Peafrancia Rural Bank of Calabanga valid and proper.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) create an inventory
committee composed of regular employees whose only duties or functions shall be to
conduct on a full time basis, an actual physical count of PPEs for a specified period of
time, to facilitate proper accounting and reporting of fixed properties; and (b)
designate one personnel each from MTO and Accounting Office who shall conduct
the reconciliation of respective records to present the accurate value of PPE in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the following measures to
ensure correctness of the Road Networks account:
a) create an Inventory Committee, preferably composed one personnel each from
the municipal Planning and Development Office; Engineering Office;
Treasurer’s Office; General Services Office; Accountant’s Office and Assessor
Office who shall conduct, on a full time basis and within specified time,
inspection and inventory of all road networks;
b) require the created inventory committee to conduct a physical
count/inventory of all the municipal roads and reconcile it with the Municipal
Road Map, if any and determine the existence and condition of the road
components of each road for proper measurement and valuation using the
prescribed forms such as: a) Local Road and Bridge Inventory and Condition
Survey; b) Report of Local Road Network; and c) Report on the Physical
Count of Local Road Network;
c) direct the Municipal Accounting Office to: gather all details and information
needed to determine the cost of the road lot, road components and
depreciable life of each road network and reconcile it with the recorded cost in
48
the Registry of Public Infrastructure and existing ledgers; prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the recognition of Public Infrastructure
- Roads, Highways and Bridges in the General Ledger amounting to
₱27,895,936.71 as of December 31, 2019 ensuring that the accounting and
reporting procedures laid down under COA Circular 2015-008 are fully
complied by the concerned offices while the same procedure shall be
undertaken for other public infrastructures accounts;
d) instruct the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office to
strictly prepare and maintain all the prescribed reports under COA Circular
2015-008;
e) require the Municipal Planning and Development Office to maintain the
information on the local road network of the MGG, particularly, the plans and
programs of the construction, repair and maintenance of local roads and the
depreciable life of the road networks in coordination with the Engineering
Office;
f) direct the Municipal Engineering Office to ensure that the Local Road
Network map is aligned to the requirement of a road map described under the
DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2017-159 and Local Roads Assets
Management Manual for LGUs; and, as the Office that undertakes the
construction and repair of the local roads, documents relative thereto shall be
maintained so that the necessary and detailed information of a certain road
project, such as complete description of the road and the cost segregation of
the road components are available anytime needed by the accounting and
general services office and other stakeholders;
g) require the Municipal Assessor’s Office to prepare and maintain the tax maps,
which is used as basis for identifying the ownership of all the road lots in the of
municipal road network and shall also assist in determining the cost of the
road lots with no available costs; and
h) instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to gather and keep in her vault
documents regarding the ownership or acquisition of road land or road right
of way through purchase, donation or any other modes of acquisition.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accounting Office
to:
a) gather the details/information records, and other alternative documents
needed to determine the components of local roads identified in the inventory;
b) segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with
identified cost and recognize component without corresponding cost at its fair
value;
c) depreciate the carrying amount of existing roads and its components to reflect
the asset in its used condition and shall serve as the basis for recording in the
general ledger;
d) determine the life of the local roads within the range prescribed by COA
taking into consideration the following factors: a. design b. users c. volume of
users;
e) compute separately the depreciation for the road components taking into
consideration the initial cost and useful life.
f) ensure that the details and information must be indicated in the Property,
Plant and Equipment Ledger Card - Local Road Network Ledger Card
prepared and maintained for each item of road network;
g) re-compute the accumulated depreciation and depreciation expense from CY
2016 to CY 2019 and thereafter adjust the affected accounts and the net book
value of each of the asset; and
h) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the year-end balance of the
Public Infrastructure and Government Equity accounts.

We recommended that Management:
a. direct the Municipal Accounting Office to closely monitor that all withheld
amount due to the GSIS, HDMF and PHIC are entirely/fully remitted to
avoid penalties, charges and sanctions that may be imposed against the
Municipality and avoid inconvenience to employees on the availment of
benefits from the said corporate agencies;
b. coordinate with GSIS on the difference of the amount billed against the
withheld and remitted balance to avoid incurrence and pile up of
difference; and
c. initiate a monthly reconciliation of amount withheld and remitted against
the records of GSIS, HDMF and PHIC to ensure that all withheld
premiums and loan payments are fully remitted at the end of the year.

We recommended that Management:
a) continue to trace and retrieve relevant documents to serve as basis in
analyzing prior and current year reconciling items and prepare necessary
adjustment, if any;
b) conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to eliminate the
difference and effect any adjustment if any.
c) strictly adhere to COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 or the
Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units and pertinent
provisions of P.D. 1445 on the proper account identification, classification and
recording.

We recommended that Management require the responsible officials to submit
the lacking documents supporting the contract and the payment of claims for the
various infrastructure projects with total contract costs of ₱35,993,474.29 as well as
other documents needed for contract and technical review.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that liquidated damages
are deducted from the final payment of the contractors who failed to complete
infrastructure projects within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management:
a) direct the Lead Offices or implementers of GAD Activities to fully utilize
the GAD Funds or re-program funds through a revised GPB in case funds
were shouldered by other LGUs or NGAs in order to provide GAD-related
services amounting to at least 5 per cent of the annual budget;
b) enjoin the MPDO to spearhead the preparation and completion of the
Municipality’s GAD Database which will aid in the gender analysis and
full utilization of GAD Funds for existing and emerging GAD issues; and
c) devise an applicable scheme wherein the list of disbursements charged
against the GAD Fund may be provided by the Municipal Accounting
Office to the Audit Team, decision-makers and other stakeholders.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that all disclosure
requirement in the Notes to the Financial Statements be included to present complete
and relevant information to fairly present the financial position and performance of
the Municipal Government of Gainza.

We recommended that Management: (a) to refrain from charging against the
Local Development Fund, programs, projects or activities not included in the Local
Development Plan for the budget year; and (b) that an in-depth study and planning
be conducted to ensure feasible implementation of the identified PPAs for the year to
achieve optimal utilization of the Fund.

We recommended that Management facilitate the appointment of personnel of
the LDRRMO for at least four (4) statutory positions mandated under R.A. 10121
and strictly adhere to the policies and guidelines regarding the: a) preparation of
LDRRMFIP; b) preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF;
and c) disclosure of unexpended amount of the Fund in the Notes to the Financial
Statements as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor, in his capacity as the Chairman
of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, facilitate the establishment of fixed
Material Recovery Facilities (MRFs) on the concerned 7 barangays and ensure strict
adherence to the provisions of R.A. 9003 to promote environmental protection.

We recommended that Management:
a) exert stringent efforts in the collection of local taxes from its constituents;
b) tap all possible sources of income in order to substantially augment the
revenues that can be used for the financial operation of the Municipality;
and
c) remind taxpayers of their reciprocal obligations to pay their taxes on time
which are ultimately given back by the government in the form of
benefits/services.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain an updated Cashbook for Cash in Bank-LCCA account and make it readily available for inspection anytime during office hours ; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records and observe the highest standards of prudence and consistency in the keeping of accounts as safeguard against inaccurate or misleading information to stakeholders.

We recommended that Management:

require the Accountable Officers concerned to settle/liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances and the concerned officials to adhere strictly to the rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;
instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the causes of negative ending balances of cash advance accounts and perform necessary actions on the correction of such negative balances; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant on the issuance of demand letters to the Accountable Officers on the immediate liquidation of their outstanding cash advances, especially to separated officers with outstanding cash advances; and
ensure that outstanding cash advances are liquidated before approval of separation from office of any officer or employee or cessation of an elected official’s term.

require the Municipal Accountant to identify the causes  of the variance of the unreconciled reciprocal accounts, and thereafter cause the recording of the necessary adjustments; and
strictly adhere to COA Circular 2015-009  dated December 1, 2015 or the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units and pertinent provisions of P.D. 1445 on the proper account identification,  classification and recording.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require:

the Inventory Committee to hasten the completion of the actual physical count of all the PPEs to facilitate correct updating of the RPCPPE and acknowledgement records;
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their respective records of PPE; and
the officers concerned to immediately dispose unserviceable properties before such properties lose their value to a significant amount.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

attach a schedule or computation to the vouchers of adjusting journal entries totaling ₱608,934.13 representing the withheld taxes by SEF, TF and 20%DF; and
trace the causes of the negative ending balance of the Due to BIR account amounting to ₱214,964.49 to validate whether entries were twice recorded or omitted.

monitor the compliance of the Municipality’s posting obligations on all its procurements in accordance with the required timelines and ensure that the BAC Certification of Posting is attached to contracts and based on printout copy of PhilGEPS posting; and
comply with the posting requirements on all notices to bid/requests for quotations, awards, results of bidding and related information to be posted in the PhilGEPS website.

allocate the required one per cent of IRA for the Local Council for the Protection of Children as mandated by Section 15 of Republic Act No 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120;
require the institution of clearly defined plan, programs and activities intended for children beneficiaries with corresponding fund allocation out of the mandated one per cent of IRA before the start of the year; and
design mechanisms for the proper utilization of funds and evaluation of implemented programs funds to be in place for easy evaluation by stakeholders whether the LCPC funds indeed catered for the children’s welfare.

require the LSB Co-Chairman and other officials concerned on the compliance with the indicative timelines provided in the Joint Circular No. 2017-1, commencing with the submission of a report on the abovementioned data to the LSB and DepEd Central Office not later than March 31 of the current year to aid the DepEd PSDS in her timely preparation of SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1; and
